summary: Iter-139 downstream MUA validation — Case Report PDF export + AI context. 5/5 pytest tests PASSED against live backend (https://dental-workflow-18.preview.emergentagent.com). Coverage: (1) Full-arch Immediate Loading + MUA='yes' + 4 per-tooth rows: POST /api/procedures/{id}/case-report returns valid application/pdf bytes containing 'Multi-unit Abutment Placed: Yes', 'Multi-unit Abutment Details', and a 'Tooth <pos>' row per implant with the actual angulation + cuff_height numeric values; 'Angulation' and 'Cuff Height' literal labels also appear; legacy sections (Torque, Prosthetic Component, Hybrid Prosthesis) still render. (2) MUA='no' case PDF contains 'Multi-unit Abutment Placed: No' but NOT the 'Multi-unit Abutment Details' details block — gated correctly. (3) Non-MUA single-implant case (MUA fields absent) PDF contains zero 'Multi-unit Abutment' substrings — no leak; existing Healing Abutment Cuff Height / Healing Abutment Placed sections still render. (4) AI context: imported `_build_case_context` directly and asserted it surfaces 'Multi-unit Abutment Placed: Yes' + 'MUA Details: Tooth 11: 15° / 3mm; ...' for MUA=yes, the 'No' line only for MUA=no, and zero MUA strings for absent. (5) Delayed Loading full-arch with numeric (int) MUA values still renders correctly in PDF — confirms numeric stringification works.
critical_code_review_comments: GOOD: PDF generator (server.py L5621-5639) gates the MUA block on `mua_placed in ('yes','no')` so absent/null does NOT leak — verified by test_03. The details sub-block is further gated on `mua_placed == 'yes' and isinstance(mua_details, list) and mua_details` so MUA=no skips the per-tooth rows — verified by test_02.

GOOD: PDF row format `Tooth {tooth}:  Angulation {ang}°   Cuff Height {cuff} mm` keeps literal labels 'Angulation' and 'Cuff Height' so PyPDF2 text extraction can assert on them even after the latin-1 safe() sanitiser strips the degree symbol (°). Test asserts on numeric value + label rather than the symbol — robust to encoding.

GOOD: AI context (_build_case_context, server.py L4475-4490) mirrors the same gate (`mua_placed in ('yes','no')`) and adds the same numeric placeholders to the LLM prompt. Direct unit test (no DB / no LLM call) confirms the helper is a pure function — fast, deterministic, recommended pattern for future iterations.

MINOR (informational, not a bug): The PDF safe() sanitiser does encode-replace via latin-1 so `°` becomes `?` in the binary stream. Already acknowledged in the spec ('Accept either ° or no degree symbol'). If you ever migrate to a Unicode-aware font (e.g., DejaVu via fpdf.add_font), the degree symbol will render natively without code changes — the gate logic is encoding-agnostic.

MINOR (informational, not a bug): The empty-row guard inside the for-loop (`if not isinstance(row, dict): continue`) silently skips malformed rows. Matches the spec's non-blocking validation philosophy. No action needed.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_mua_pdf_export_iteration139.py (NEW — 5 pytest cases, ~290 LOC; reuses helpers from sibling test_mua_phase2_iteration139.py for case creation, Phase 1 approval, consent upload, and module-scope cleanup)
test_credentials: Used Gaurav.pandey@student.dental.edu / Student@123 (student), Paresh.gandhi@dental.edu / Supervisor@123 (supervisor), Abhijit.patil@dental.edu / Admin@123 (incharge). All from /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created 4 ephemeral procedures (2 All-on-4 Immediate Loading for tests 01/02, 1 Single Conventional Implant for test 03, 1 All-on-4 Delayed Loading for test 05). All deleted in module-scope cleanup fixture; test_04 needs no DB/network seed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend MUA persistence (iter 128) AND downstream PDF + AI context (this iter) are fully verified. For frontend: read-only MUA card lives in /app/frontend/app/procedures/[id].tsx around line 1573 with data-testid='mua-readonly-section' and 'mua-readonly-row-<idx>' — gated on procedure.phase2_data.multi_unit_abutment_placed in ('yes','no'). Pattern for any future PDF tests: POST /api/procedures/{id}/case-report (NOT GET, not /case-report-pdf — actual route is /case-report), assert content-type starts with application/pdf, parse with PyPDF2.PdfReader. Note: PDF safe() sanitiser strips '°' to '?' under latin-1, so always assert on numeric values + literal 'Angulation'/'Cuff Height' labels rather than the degree symbol. Tests run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL set explicitly: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://dental-workflow-18.preview.emergentagent.com EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=... pytest tests/test_mua_pdf_export_iteration139.py -v`.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Initial pytest collection failed because tests/test_mua_phase2_iteration139.py reads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL OR EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL at module-import time and there is no /app/backend/.env REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — the backend container only sees EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL inside frontend/.env. Resolved by exporting REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<public URL> in the pytest invocation. Same pattern documented for next agent.
